title: Use Case
description: A reusable application pattern: how a class of EO data/services addresses a class of user needs for a market segment. The use case is the hub of the graph - it 'addresses' needs/requirements and is 'instantiated' by concrete case studies. Unlike a case study it is generalised (not one real deployment) and unlike a need it is solution-oriented (the how, not the why).

title.description: Reader-facing headline, e.g. 'Monitoring infrastructure stability with InSAR ground motion'.

description.description: The full use-case body: the problem context, how EO addresses it, and the value delivered. Supports Markdown.

value_proposition.description: One- or two-sentence plain-text statement of the core benefit of this use case. Drives hero callouts and graph node previews.

hero_image.description: Path or URL to the primary image, used in listing cards and social-share previews.

reading_time_minutes.description: Estimated reading time shown to the visitor.

actors.description: Abstract roles/personas that participate in this use case (e.g. 'bridge asset manager', 'rail infrastructure manager'). For named real-world people use a case study's stakeholders instead.

preconditions.description: Assumptions / enabling conditions that must hold for this use case to apply, e.g. 'AOI has Sentinel-1 coverage with adequate revisit', 'reference benchmarks available'.

steps.description: The EO value chain for this use case, from data to decision. Ordered, lightweight; not a full process model.

title.description: Short step label, e.g. 'Ingest Sentinel-1 SLC stack'.

description.description: What happens in this step. Supports Markdown.

products.description: Product ids (from vocab/products.json) used in this step.

benefits.description: Expected / potential outcomes of applying this use case (the case-study analogue is 'key_results', which are demonstrated).

tags.description: Free-text keyword tags for search and related-content suggestions, independent of the structured classification facets.

relations.description: Typed links. Most importantly 'addresses' -> need/requirement (what user demand this use case meets). Also 'depends_on', 'related', 'supersedes'. Case studies declare the 'instantiates' edge from their side.
